response.justification: Maria Rodriguez has extensive experience in software development across multiple domains, languages, and architectures, with a strong focus on performance and testability. However, her primary expertise lies in web, mobile, and embedded systems (Node.js, TypeScript, D, Java), with limited recent C++ experience (last C++ work was in 2015 at National Instruments). Her lack of direct industrial automation or cloud platform experience (e.g., Azure) and no clear exposure to IEC 62443 or secure development lifecycle practices for industrial systems raises concerns about her fit for this role.
strengths: Strong background in high-performance, concurrent, and event-driven architectures (e.g., real-time systems with Dlang, low-latency apps with async I/O).
Experience with modular, loosely-coupled systems and message-driven architectures (e.g., micro-services infrastructure handling millions of requests).
Advocacy for secure coding practices (e.g., TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development) and mentorship (e.g., Refactoring Dojos, Code Kata sessions).
concerns: Limited recent C++ experience (last significant work in 2015) and no evident expertise in STL or object-oriented C++ design for this role.
No direct experience with industrial automation protocols (e.g., Modbus, OPC UA) or IEC 62443 cybersecurity standards.
Most recent cloud experience is with AWS CDK and React/Node.js, with no mention of Microsoft Azure or industrial IoT/cloud platform solutions.

key_factors.factor: C++ expertise and recent hands-on experience
Industrial automation or IoT platform experience
Cloud platform (Azure) and secure development lifecycle exposure
